title: Coverage, Thresholds, and Meaningful Evidence · Guided Gate Build
summary: Set coverage policy that exposes risk without encouraging empty tests. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a changed-code coverage and exception policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret line, branch, function, condition, and path coverage limitations.
Choose global, package, and changed-code thresholds from risk and baseline.
Exclude generated or unreachable code with reviewed narrow rules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a gates-coverage-review-rev1kp: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Coverage Review into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-coverage-review-rev1kp:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Coverage Review structural check is only one gate. a changed-code coverage and exception policy must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

stimulus.title: Coverage Review failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: The highest-coverage test suite is automatically best.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pair coverage gaps with mutation, defect, and risk evidence.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Coverage Review condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Coverage Review output and hide the violated a changed-code coverage and exception policy assumption instead of explaining the failure. This does not resolve test an acceptance boundary.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the workshop evidence note for Coverage Review: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Use a changed workshop case for gates coverage that tests this competency: Pair coverage gaps with mutation, defect, and risk evidence.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
steps.why: Retrieving Coverage Review through the concrete a changed-code coverage and exception policy case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Pair coverage gaps with mutation, defect, and risk evidence.
